description: Record the signal input into a audio file slot

parameterGroups.label: Recording
parameterGroups.rationale: State controls record on/off; RecordingLength sets the duration to record

gaps.question: How does recording work? Does core.recorder write incoming audio samples to the AudioFile slot's buffer? Does it pass audio through unchanged while recording?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether the recorder is transparent (passthrough) or consumes the signal.

gaps.question: RecordingLength defaults to 0. Does 0 mean 'record until stopped' (State=0) or 'do not record'? What happens when the recording reaches the specified length?
gaps.context: The default of 0ms is unusual and needs clarification.

gaps.question: State has an encoded TextToValueConverter suggesting named values. Is it simply Off/On (0/1), or are there other states like 'armed' or 'overdub'?
gaps.context: Users need to know the recording state machine.

gaps.question: How does the recorded audio interact with the AudioFile slot? Does it resize the buffer, or must the buffer be pre-allocated? Can the recording be played back via core.file_player?
gaps.context: The workflow for recording and playback needs to be documented.

gaps.question: Does core.recorder record all input channels or just channel 0? Does it record mono or stereo depending on the input?
gaps.context: Determines whether stereo recording is supported.

notes: core.recorder writes audio input to an AudioFile slot. The State parameter has an encoded text converter, suggesting named on/off labels. RecordingLength is in milliseconds (Time converter) with a max of 2000ms (2 seconds). The default of 0 for both State and RecordingLength needs C++ exploration to understand the intended workflow. A recorder.png image is available in resources/images/. This is the counterpart to core.file_player.
